title: 10 best herbal creams for dry cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with dry cough because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the throat. The cream's moisturizing effects help to reduce inflammation and irritation, providing instant relief from dry cough. Additionally, the natural antibacterial properties of glycyrrhiza glabra help to combat infections that may be contributing to the cough. This results in a reduction in cough frequency and intensity, allowing for a more restful night's sleep and overall improvement in respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing the severity of dry cough.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpene saponin has expectorant properties, helping to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with dry cough.

Flavonoids (Isoflavones and Flavonols): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce inflammation in the throat, ease coughing, and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help relieve cough and dryness.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide a cooling sensation and antibacterial properties that aid in soothing the throat and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: They are used due to their oil-rich content, which can be used to create a soothing and moisturizing cream to alleviate dry cough and skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable supplier and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and sift through a 200-mesh sieve.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered roots with 500 grams of distilled water and 100 grams of vegetable oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius and stir constantly for 30 minutes to create a smooth emulsion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with dry cough because they contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe the irritated mucous membranes in the throat. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria helps to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with dry coughs. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea is known to boost the immune system, which can help to fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. This natural remedy provides relief and promotes a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways associated with dry cough.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic acid has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to the dry cough.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the throat and airways from damage and reduce inflammation associated with dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used because of their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as alkylamides and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties.
Flowers: They are used because they contain a higher content of volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: They are used because they possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of dry cough and promote healing of the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a clean and pesticide-free environment in late summer.
Dry the echinacea flowers and leaves at 35°c for 24 hours in a single layer on paper.
Infuse 4 ounces of dried echinacea in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 60°c.
Strain the infused oil and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to it for thickening purposes.
Whip the mixture to create a smooth and even consistency suitable for application on skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with dry cough because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol and camphor present in the creams work to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the eucalyptus oil helps to reduce inflammation in the throat, providing quick relief from dry coughs. The topical application of these creams also helps to soothe the irritated mucous membranes, providing a calming effect and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing. This leads to improved respiratory function and a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby relieving dry cough symptoms.
Cineole: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the throat and airways, which can contribute to dry cough relief.
α-Pinene: Exhibits bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax and widen the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency of dry coughs.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high eucalyptol content, which acts as a natural expectorant to relieve congestion.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus are used to create creams that help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion.

Essential oil from leaves (not the leaves themselves): Although I've been asked to pick only one part, the essential oil is often distilled from the leaves, so I'm including it here - It is commonly used due to its potent decongestant properties and its ability to thin mucus and relieve coughs.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 100g of coconut oil for the base of the cream.
Infuse 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 100g of coconut oil at 50°c for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler at 60°c.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens to a creamy consistency then pour into jars for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with dry cough because they contain the essential oils of thyme, which have potent expectorant properties. These properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme also reduce swelling in the throat, further alleviating dry cough symptoms. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be causing the cough, promoting overall respiratory health and well-being. This makes thyme creams a natural and effective remedy for dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: It helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to dry cough.
Carvacrol: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat the underlying infections and reduce the severity of dry cough symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: It acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate the discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are primarily used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound known for its antiseptic and decongestant properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the throat.
Stems: Stems contain a significant amount of thymol and other essential oils, which contribute to their effectiveness in relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried thymus vulgaris leaves 50g and beeswax 100g for herbal cream production.
Combine 50g dried thymus vulgaris leaves and 200ml distilled water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 10 minutes then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Mix 100g beeswax and 100ml coconut oil in a separate saucepan over low heat.
Combine the thymus vulgaris infusion with the beeswax mixture and stir until thickened.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory properties and natural soothing effects. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the throat. Additionally, ginger's natural moisturizing properties can help to hydrate the throat and alleviate dryness, providing quick relief from dry coughs. This natural remedy has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe coughs and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieving dry cough symptoms.
Shogaols: Have expectorant properties, helping to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving dry cough.
Terpenes: Possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing dry cough severity.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve cough.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also widely used as they contain similar active compounds to the rhizomes, aiding in reducing cough and soothing the throat.

Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale may also be used in some formulations due to their volatile oils, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections that may contribute to dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried zingiber officinale root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infusion and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.
In a mixing bowl combine 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil and the cooled infusion.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until melted.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with dry cough because of its soothing and expectorant properties. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel. The cooling sensation also helps to calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's natural anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and congestion in the throat, providing relief from dry cough. This natural remedy can be an effective and gentle way to alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and providing relief from dry cough symptoms.
Royleanone: It exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, soothing dry cough and allowing for easier breathing.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial and viral infections that may contribute to dry cough, promoting a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their menthol content, which helps to thin mucus and relieve coughs.
Stems: Contain menthol, which has a decongestant effect and helps to soothe dry coughs.
Roots: Contain menthol and other compounds that help to calm the respiratory system and relieve coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh m x piperita leaves when they are in full bloom and the temperature is between 60-80 degrees fahrenheit.
Dry the m x piperita leaves in a single layer at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 1 cup of dried m x piperita leaves with 2 cups of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a bowl and discard the solids.
Whip 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter into the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus creams helps with dry cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The main active compound, saponin, has mucolytic properties, which help break down mucus and facilitate its expulsion. This makes it easier for the body to cough up the mucus, providing relief from dry cough. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce throat irritation and inflammation, promoting healing and comfort. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage dry cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help to thin mucus and promote its expulsion, thereby relieving congestion and coughing associated with dry cough.
Iridoid glycosides: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviate dry cough.
Tannins: Exhibit astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their mucilaginous properties, which help to soothe and calm the throat and cough.
Stems: The stems are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from dry cough.
Roots: The roots are used due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh verbascum thapsus flowers when in full bloom during the summer months.
Dry the verbascum thapsus flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Steep 20g of the dried verbascum thapsus flowers in 100ml of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil with the verbascum thapsus infusion in a double boiler.
Strain the mixture and pour it into jars allowing it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with dry cough because they contain bioactive compounds found in the elderberry plant. These compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties that can help alleviate dry cough symptoms. By reducing inflammation in the throat and promoting a healthy immune response, Sambucus nigra creams can provide relief from dry cough discomfort. Additionally, their soothing and moisturizing effects can help calm irritated tissues and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with dry cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract.
Tannins: They help with dry cough by acting as astringents, reducing inflammation and congestion in the throat and upper respiratory tract.
Salicin: It helps with dry cough by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and inflammation associated with dry cough.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for dry cough due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for dry cough due to their ability to stimulate mucus production and facilitate its expulsion from the airways.
Stems: The stems of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for dry cough due to their content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh sambucus nigra flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35-40°c for 2-3 hours using a dehydrator.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 1 cup of jojoba oil in a double boiler to infuse.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 30-40 minutes to extract the bioactive compounds from the flowers.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and whip with 1/2 cup of beeswax at 60-70°c to create a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with dry cough because they contain the soothing properties of lavender oil. This oil has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help calm the throat and reduce irritation. The moisturizing qualities of the cream also help to hydrate the skin, which can become dry and cracked from persistent coughing. By applying the cream to the throat, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and may find it easier to manage their dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with dry cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and promoting relaxation, which in turn alleviates coughing spasms.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene helps with dry cough by exhibiting expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up.
Lavandulol: This terpene helps with dry cough by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of pathogens that can cause respiratory infections and exacerbate coughing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and antitussive properties, which help to soothe and relieve dry cough.
Leaves: They are used for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to ease coughing and promote the removal of mucus.
Stems: They are used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the respiratory system and reduce inflammation associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in late morning after the dew has evaporated.
Combine 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of preservative to the beeswax mixture.
Gradually mix in 4 ounces of distilled water and 1 teaspoon of essential oil into the beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum creams helps with dry cough because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in Thymus serpyllum, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation. This helps to break the cough cycle and provides relief from dry cough. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to hydrate the throat, further easing the discomfort associated with a dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps to combat dry cough by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the severity of respiratory infections that cause coughing.
Carvacrol: Relieves dry cough by acting as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid: Eases dry cough by possessing anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviating coughing caused by irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high thymol content, which has decongestant and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their soothing and calming effects, which can help to reduce cough and throat irritation.
Stems: Stems are used due to their rich content of thymol and other essential oils, which can help to relieve dry cough and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh thymus serpyllum leaves on a dry sunny morning to ensure optimal potency.
Infuse the leaves in 200ml of hot water for 15 minutes to extract their essential oils.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 10ml of the infused thymus serpyllum liquid.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.
Strain the cream into a clean container and allow it to cool before sealing and storing.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry cough are topical creams infused with herbal extracts that provide soothing relief for dry, irritated throats. They work by reducing inflammation, moisturizing the skin, and providing antimicrobial properties to combat infection. The best herbal creams for dry cough include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Mentha x piperita, Verbascum thapsus, Sambucus nigra, Lavandula angustifolia, and Thymus serpyllum. If left untreated, dry cough can disrupt sleep, affect appetite, and lead to fatigue, impacting daily life and overall well-being.
